name: Halloween Pumpkin Pie
recipeIngredient: 2 lb sugar pumpkin or pie pumpkin
1/4 tsp ground ginger or freshly grated ginger root
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse stem and halve the pumpkin. Scrape away seeds and filaments.
2. Cut the pumpkin into 2-inch chunks.
3. Remove the skin using a paring knife.
4. Place the pumpkin in a large baking dish and add 1/2 cup water. Cover tightly with foil.
5. Bake the pumpkin at 350°F on the middle oven rack until soft, about 1 hour, adding more water if needed.
6. Cool and puree the pumpkin in a food processor.
7. Scrape the pumpkin puree or canned pumpkin into a bowl.
8. Whisk in eggs and yolks.
9. Add sugar, salt, cinnamon, ginger, nutmeg, and half and half, whisking smooth between each addition.
10. Pour the filling into the prepared pie crust.
11. Bake at 350°F on the lowest oven rack until the crust is baked through and the filling is set, about 1 hour.
12. Cool the pie on a rack. If the pie must sit more than two hours before serving, loosely cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate.
Note: Presented by Nick Malgieri, L.A. Times article "Beyond Jack O'Lanterns", 10/27/94, page H26. For best results, use an orange-fleshed sugar pumpkin or pie pumpkin, available at farmer's markets and roadside stands in the fall. Do not use the common supermarket jack-o-lantern type pumpkin, as it is watery and insipid when cooked. You may also substitute cooked sweet potatoes or acorn squash for the pumpkin.
